perturbation.original_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder covenant and agree that each shall not for a period of one (1) year, communicate or divulge to, or use for the benefit of itself or any other person, firm, association or corporation, any information in any way relating to the Proprietary Property, in competition with the business of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder covenant and agree that each shall not for a period of one (1) year, communicate or divulge to, or use for the benefit of itself or any other person, firm, association or corporation, any information in any way relating to the Proprietary Property, in competition with the business of the Company, unless the Purchaser provides written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a condition to the non-disclosure agreement, stating that disclosure is permissible with the Purchaser's written consent. This creates a contradiction with the later clause defining remedies for breach of the non-disclosure agreement, because it introduces an exception where previously there was none, thus impacting the agreement in Section 3(d)(ii)(aa).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (ii) Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder expressly agree that in the event of any suit which may be brought by the Company for any violation of the provisions of this Agreement, any such breach or threatened breach of this Agreement shall entitle the Company to any and/or of the following remedies: (aa) an order in any such suit enjoining Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder from violating said provisions. An order to that effect may be entered at any stage of such litigation, without the requirement to post bond, and any application for such injunction shall be without prejudice to any other right of action which may accrue to the parties by reason of the breach or threatened breach of this Agreement; and (bb) an order in any such suit providing for monetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposed a strict one-year non-disclosure agreement. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing disclosure with the Purchaser's consent. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that any breach or threatened breach of the agreement entitles the Company to an injunction. The exception created by the changed text means that disclosure with consent is no longer a breach, creating a direct conflict with the absolute terms of the contradicted section, making enforcement of the original non-disclosure agreement unclear. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' type because the modification creates a conflicting implication regarding remedies for breach.

perturbation.original_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, expressly covenant and agree that for a period of one (1) year, Seller will not engage in any business or perform any service, directly or indirectly, in competition with the business of the Company, or have any interest, whether as proprietor, partner, employee, stockholder, principal, agent, consultant, director, officer, or in any other capacity or manner whatsoever, in any enterprise that shall engage in the business of the Company, except through publicly-traded shares of a corporation or mutual fund listed on a major stock exchange.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder, jointly and severally, expressly covenant and agree that for a period of one (1) year, Seller will not engage in any business or perform any service, directly or indirectly, in competition with the business of the Company, or have any interest, whether as proprietor, partner, employee, stockholder, principal, agent, consultant, director, officer, or in any other capacity or manner whatsoever, in any enterprise that shall engage in the business of the Company, except through publicly-traded shares of a corporation or mutual fund listed on a major stock exchange, provided that such interest does not exceed 5% of the total outstanding shares.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause prohibits competition except through publicly-traded shares. Adding the condition that such interest 'does not exceed 5% of the total outstanding shares' introduces a conflict. It's now unclear if holding more than 5% constitutes a breach of the covenant not to compete, especially considering Section 3(d)(i) which states that a breach will not be adequately compensated by money damages, in which introduces uncertainty to a remedy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (i) Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder understand that the provisions of this Agreement contain restrictive covenants and prohibit the disclosure of the Proprietary Property of the Company, agree to the reasonability of said provisions, and do herewith expressly agree and acknowledge that their breach of this Agreement will not be adequately compensated by money damages. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder acknowledge that the restrictions contained in this Agreement are a reasonable and necessary protection of the legitimate interests of the Company and that any violation of these restrictions would cause substantial irreparable injury to the Company. Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder acknowledge that Purchaser would not have entered into this Agreement without receiving the consideration offered by Seller and Shareholder in binding itself to these restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition to the non-compete clause (5% ownership limit), which contradicts the original clause's broad restriction and introduces ambiguity. Section (i) states that a breach of the agreement, including the non-compete, will not be adequately compensated by money damages and cause substantial irreparable injury. The addition of the 5% threshold creates uncertainty, as it is now unclear whether exceeding the 5% threshold constitutes a breach that would trigger this section, thereby contradicting the assumption that a breach would cause irreparable harm not compensable by money, which aligns with the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The covenants on the part of Key Personnel and Shareholder contained in this Agreement shall be construed as an agreement independent of any other provision in this Agreement or the SPA. The existence of any claim or cause of action of Seller and/or Shareholder against the Company or Purchaser, whether predicated on this Agreement, the SPA or otherwise shall not constitute a defense to the enforcement of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The covenants on the part of Key Personnel and Shareholder contained in this Agreement shall be construed as an agreement independent of any other provision in this Agreement or the SPA. The existence of any claim or cause of action of Seller and/or Shareholder against the Company or Purchaser, whether predicated on this Agreement, the SPA or otherwise shall not constitute a defense to the enforcement of this Agreement, unless such claim or action directly relates to a breach of the Stock Purchase Agreement by the Purchaser.
perturbation.explanation: This addition creates a contradiction by stating that claims related to a breach of the Stock Purchase Agreement *can* be a defense, contradicting the original statement that *no* claim can be a defense. This affects the severability clause later on in Section 10, because the enforceability may not follow if they are held to be conflicting or uneforcable, which could affect the remainder of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Should any clause, sentence, paragraph, subsection, Section or Article of this Agreement be judicially declared to be invalid, unenforceable or void, such decision will not have the effect of invalidating or voiding the remainder of this Agreement, and the parties agree that the part or parts of this Agreement so held to be invalid, unenforceable or void will be deemed to have been stricken by the parties, and the remainder will have the same force and effectiveness as if such stricken part or parts had never been included herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by adding an exception: claims related to a breach of the Stock Purchase Agreement can now be a defense, whereas the original text stated that no claim could be a defense. This directly contradicts the severability clause in the contradicted text, which states that if any part of the agreement is deemed unenforceable, the remainder should still be valid. The added exception creates uncertainty about the enforceability of the agreement, which can cause a conflict between the changed text and the contradicted text and potentially cause an issue of severability, thus meaning they are contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: All notices, consents, waivers, requests and other communications under this Agreement must be in writing to: (i) SELLER/ Troy Holdings International, Inc. SHAREHOLDER/ Attn: Scott MacCannell, President KEY PERSONNEL: Ana Misra, Vice President 204-11 Cidermill Avenue Vaughan, Ontario, L4K 4B6, Canada E-mail: smaccannell@troyinc.ca amisra@troyinc.ca
perturbation.changed_text: All notices, consents, waivers, requests and other communications under this Agreement must be in writing to: (i) SELLER/ Troy Holdings International, Inc. SHAREHOLDER/ Attn: Scott MacCannell, President KEY PERSONNEL: Ana Misra, Vice President 204-11 Cidermill Avenue Vaughan, Ontario, L4K 4B6, Canada E-mail: smaccannell@troyinc.ca amisra@troyinc.ca OR via verbal communication.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that all communication *must* be in writing. By adding 'OR via verbal communication', this directly contradicts the initial requirement. This creates issues with Section 6, which outlines the need for amendments to be written, to ensure any changes made verbally would be considered void, but this change now makes it contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No amendment, modification, restatement or supplement of this Agreement shall be valid unless the same is in writing and signed by the parties hereto. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be valid unless in writing and signed by the party against whom that waiver is sought to be enforced.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that all communications 'must be in writing.' The changed text introduces an alternative: 'OR via verbal communication.' This directly conflicts with the original requirement for written communication. This contradiction is significant because the contradicted text explicitly states that 'No amendment, modification, restatement or supplement of this Agreement shall be valid unless the same is in writing'. The introduction of verbal communication undermines the enforceability of amendments and waivers, as they are required to be in writing, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement and the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, without giving effect to the conflict of law principles thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement and the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, but the parties are free to pursue claims under the laws of Ontario, Canada, if they so choose, without giving effect to the conflict of law principles thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The agreement is stated to be governed by Illinois law. By adding 'but the parties are free to pursue claims under the laws of Ontario, Canada, if they so choose' introduces direct conflict on what governing law is applicable. Now, the clause can be interpreted to enforce any judgements with conflict of law, creating doubt in Section 13 on which court the parties will irrevocably submit to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties hereby: a. irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction of any Illinois court, over any claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement and irrevocably agrees that any and all such claims may be heard and determined in such Illinois court, in and for Cook County, and b. irrevocably waives, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any objection it may now or hereafter have to the venue in any proceeding being brought in a court in the Circuit Court of Cook County, Illinois.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the option to pursue claims under Ontario law, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that stipulates exclusive jurisdiction in Illinois courts. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding which jurisdiction governs claims arising from the agreement, making the agreement unenforceable in either jurisdiction. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the clause regarding the jurisdiction is now in conflict.

perturbation.original_text: If any party commences an action against another party to enforce any of the terms, covenants, conditions or provisions of this Agreement, or because of a breach by a party of its obligations under this Agreement, the prevailing party in any such action shall be entitled to recover its losses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and interest incurred in connection with the enforcement of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: If any party commences an action against another party to enforce any of the terms, covenants, conditions or provisions of this Agreement, or because of a breach by a party of its obligations under this Agreement, the prevailing party in any such action *may*, at the court's discretion, be entitled to recover its losses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and interest incurred in connection with the enforcement of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be entitled' to 'may, at the court's discretion, be entitled' significantly weakens the prevailing party clause. The original version provides a clear entitlement to recover losses. The modified version introduces judicial discretion, making it uncertain whether the prevailing party will actually recover their losses. This introduces a conflict with Section 3(d)(ii) where the company is entitled to remedies in law or in equity, because the recovery of any potential loss in monetary damages is now not guaranteed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (ii) Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder expressly agree that in the event of any suit which may be brought by the Company for any violation of the provisions of this Agreement, any such breach or threatened breach of this Agreement shall entitle the Company to any and/or of the following remedies: (aa) an order in any such suit enjoining Seller, Key Personnel and Shareholder from violating said provisions. An order to that effect may be entered at any stage of such litigation, without the requirement to post bond, and any application for such injunction shall be without prejudice to any other right of action which may accrue to the parties by reason of the breach or threatened breach of this Agreement; and (bb) an order in any such suit providing for monetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall be entitled' to 'may, at the court's discretion, be entitled' weakens the prevailing party clause. The original version provides a clear entitlement to recover losses, whereas the changed version introduces judicial discretion, making recovery uncertain. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text which states that the Company is 'entitle[d] to any and/or of the following remedies,' including monetary damages, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
